decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who embezzled a relatively modest sum and has demonstrated remorse by initiating restitution. While embezzlement is a serious breach of fiduciary trust, a 10-year sentence suggested by the spectator is disproportionately high for a first-time non-violent financial crime. A sentence of 8 months is appropriate to reflect the severity of the betrayal of trust while accounting for the mitigating factors of a clean record and partial restitution.
